source: NEWCIS curated PNG hazard record (volcanoes, tsunamis, major disasters)

events.name: Titan Ridge (Submarine)
events.details: Underwater eruption in the Central Bismarck Sea; triggered by a 5.6 magnitude quake, sending a steam column up to 28,000 ft.

events.name: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila
events.province: Bougainville (AROB), East New Britain, Milne Bay
events.location: Outer island margins, Solomon Sea corridor, Baining Mountains
events.details: 50+ deaths. The strongest recorded cyclone in the region's history. Triggered destructive landslides in East New Britain (killing 12) and severe coastal flooding that left over 114,000 people in need of humanitarian aid.

events.name: Catastrophic Landslide
events.details: 670 to 2,000+ estimated deaths. A massive, early-morning mountainside collapse buried over 150 homes under 8 meters of rock and mud, entirely cutting off the Highlands Highway.

events.name: Highlands Flash Floods & Landslides
events.province: Western Highlands, Jiwaka, Chimbu, Eastern Highlands
events.location: Wahgi Valley and Highlands Highway corridor
events.details: 9,355+ displaced and over 50,000 affected. Days of torrential rain triggered simultaneous flash floods and landslides across four Highlands provinces, severing the Highlands Highway and destroying food gardens.

events.name: Sepik River Floods & 6.9 Earthquake
events.details: Thousands affected. A magnitude 6.9 quake struck amid severe Sepik River flooding; the Yuat and Karawari tributaries burst their banks, submerging food gardens, aid posts and houses across all five Angoram LLGs.

events.name: Gulf Coastal King-Tide Flooding
events.details: 50,000+ affected. Unusually high king tides flooded tens of coastal and downstream estuary villages, inundating staple breadfruit and sago crops and contaminating freshwater sources.

events.name: Ulawun
events.details: Explosive Plinian event; ash plume hit 50,000 ft, cutting off regional aviation corridors.

events.name: Bagana
events.details: Major explosive escalation of its ongoing effusive activity, forcing the evacuation of over 5,000 people.

events.name: Manam
events.details: Powerful paroxysm sending ash to 50,000 ft. Eruptions in 2004 forced a total island evacuation.

events.name: King-Tide Coastal Inundation
events.details: 53,000+ displaced nationwide. A surge of king tides flooded coastal villages and low-lying atolls - up to 30,000 affected in New Ireland and coastal/atoll communities of ~50,000 in Manus - inundating homes, gardens, water catchments and cemeteries.

events.name: Mumeng Landslide
events.details: 5 deaths. Heavy rain triggered a landslide that flooded the Vibo River and swept away a family of alluvial miners - three adults and two children - at their mining site.

events.name: Goilala Landslide
events.details: 13+ deaths (15 feared). A pre-dawn landslide buried an alluvial gold-mining camp between rugged mountains accessible only by helicopter, burying miners asleep in their tents.

events.name: Kadovar
events.details: Broke centuries of dormancy with an emergent ash plume, forcing a total island evacuation.

events.name: Tsunami — Island Volcanic Collapse
events.details: 0 deaths. Small, immediate wave displacement detected when the island volcano shattered its dome during its initial eruption. · max wave 1.0 m (3 ft)

events.name: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake
events.location: Epicenter in Hela; widespread throughout the Highlands
events.details: 160+ deaths. Shaking triggered hundreds of massive landslides that severed major infrastructure, dammed rivers, and destroyed rural villages, affecting half a million people.

events.name: Ok Tedi Environmental Disaster
events.details: Man-Made. Following a tailings dam failure, roughly 90 million tons of mine waste were discharged into the river annually. It contaminated 1,300 km2 of land, poisoning forests, destroying fisheries, and ruining the livelihoods of 50,000 people.

events.name: Tumbi Landslide
events.details: 25+ deaths. A massive limestone hillside failure near an LNG quarry swept down and completely buried a village under a 10-meter wall of debris.

events.name: Cyclone Guba
events.details: 150 deaths. Torrential rains caused catastrophic flash flooding. Major rivers burst their banks, washing away bridges and completely isolating the province for weeks.

events.name: Garbuna Group
events.details: Woke up unexpectedly after 1,300 years of silence, venting ash and covering local plantations.

events.name: Pago
events.details: Major effusive fracturing event that lasted into 2003, producing massive lava flows and closing the local airport.

events.name: Tsunami — 7.6 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 0 deaths. Destroyed hundreds of coastal homes and coastal infrastructure, but local communities self-evacuated inland immediately. · max wave 5.5 m (18 ft)

events.name: Tsunami — 8.0 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 0 deaths. Occurred during a massive multi-fault rupture sequence, causing deep coastal inundation across the islands. · max wave 3.0 m (10 ft)

events.name: Tsunami — Earthquake & Undersea Landslide
events.details: 2,205 deaths. PNG’s worst tsunami. A 7.0 quake triggered a catastrophic undersea sediment slump, completely vaporizing coastal villages along a 14km stretch. · max wave 15.0 m (49 ft)

events.name: El Niño Drought & Frosts
events.details: Hundreds of indirect deaths. Severe drought dried up rivers, while extreme night frosts wiped out staple sweet potato (kaukau) crops, leaving 40% of the population facing severe food shortages and malnutrition.

events.name: Tavurvur (Rabaul)
events.details: Last major blast was in 2014. Famously erupted simultaneously with Vulcan in 1994.

events.name: Tsunami — Dual Volcanic Eruption
events.details: 0 deaths. Water displaced inside the caldera by the twin eruptions of Vulcan & Tavurvur smashed piers and tossed boats ashore. · max wave 3.0 m (10 ft)

events.name: Long Island (Motmot Vent)
events.details: The small intra-caldera cone Motmot erupted, throwing up ash and building up the lake island.

events.name: Karkar
events.details: Sudden phreatic (steam) explosion inside the inner caldera that killed two monitoring volcanologists.

events.name: Tsunami — 7.9 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 0 deaths. Triggered localized, non-lethal coastal flooding along the southern boundaries of Bougainville Island. · max wave 2.0 m (6.5 ft)

events.name: Ritter Island
events.details: Submarine explosive event that produced minor tsunamis and localized ash plumes.

events.name: Tsunami — Submarine Volcanic Eruption
events.details: 0 deaths. Seafloor collapse and explosive underwater venting smashed local transport canoes and fishing property. · max wave 1.5 m (5 ft)

events.name: Langila
events.details: One of the most active cyclic eruptors in the Pacific; has maintained near-continuous low-level explosions for over 50 years.

events.name: Tsunami — 8.1 Magnitude Earthquake
events.location: Pomio Coast, Jaquinot Bay, Rabaul Harbour
events.details: 0 deaths. Part of a great doublet earthquake sequence. Waves heavily damaged localized plantations, wharves, and shoreline assets. · max wave 10.0 m (33 ft)

events.name: Tsunami — 8.0 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 1 death. Preceded the July 26 event by 12 days; flooded shoreline businesses, roads, and residential foundations. · max wave 6.0 m (20 ft)

events.name: Tsunami — 6.9 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 3 deaths (via tsunami). A sudden recession of water capsized sea canoes. Underwater landslides also snapped the SEACOM communications cable. · max wave 3.0 m (10 ft)

events.name: Bam
events.details: Scored several explosive bursts throughout the early 1950s, peaking with severe ash falls in 1954.

events.name: Mount Lamington
events.details: Lethal, unpredicted pyroclastic flows collapsed the dome, destroying Higaturu and killing roughly 3,000 people.

events.name: Waiowa (Goropu)
events.details: A brand new pyroclastic cone that burst through the earth, erupting aggressively through August 1944.

events.name: Vulcan (Rabaul)
events.details: Erupted simultaneously alongside Tavurvur on both dates (1937 and 1994) before going quiet.

events.name: Tsunami — Dual Volcanic Eruption
events.details: Unspecified deaths. The explosive birth of the Vulcan cone displaced the harbor basin, compounding the 500+ deaths caused by volcanic ash. · max wave 2.5 m (8 ft)

events.name: Tsunami — 6.5 Magnitude Earthquake
events.details: 27 deaths. A violent localized displacement in the Bismarck Sea caught coastline populations completely unawares. · max wave 12.0 m (39 ft)
